text: Tim Berners-Lee invented the World Wide Web at CERN in 1989.
Grace Hopper developed the first compiler, A-0, at Remington Rand in 1952.
Marie Curie discovered polonium and radium in Paris in 1898.
Linus Torvalds released the Linux kernel from Helsinki in 1991.
Ada Lovelace wrote the first computer algorithm for the Analytical Engine in 1843.
Alan Turing proposed the Turing machine model in his 1936 paper in Cambridge.
Steve Wozniak designed the Apple I personal computer in Cupertino in 1976.
Margaret Hamilton led the Apollo flight software team at MIT during the 1960s.
